trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at 11.7ms, optimized to 7.31ms (score 65.10) using torch.compile and float32 matmul precision. Profiling shows bmm ~40%, mm ~35%, elementwise ~25%. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You've spent significant time debugging a custom Triton attention kernel without even a baseline score. Start simpler: create a `ModelNew` that uses `nn.MultiheadAttention` and `nn.LayerNorm` directly (matching the reference exactly), run the benchmark to get a baseline score, then optimize incrementally. You can try `F.scaled_dot_product_attention` or `torch.compile` as first optimization levers before writing custom Triton kernels.

Stop reimplementing `nn.MultiheadAttention` initialization from scratch. Instead, just **use** `nn.MultiheadAttention` and `nn.LayerNorm` inside your `ModelNew.__init__` — the test harness seeds RNG identically for both models, so identical module constructors produce identical weights. Your `forward()` can then call `self.attn(x, x, x)` exactly like the reference. Get a baseline score first, then optimize the forward path (e.g., `torch.compile`, `F.scaled_dot_product_attention`).

Score 64.9 is a good start but there's likely more room. Try `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")` instead of `mode="default"`, and also try writing a custom `forward` that uses `F.scaled_dot_product_attention` directly while keeping `nn.MultiheadAttention` in `__init__` for weight compatibility (extract `self.attn.in_proj_weight`, split into Q/K/V projections, use SDPA, then output projection). This avoids the initialization mismatch you hit earlier.
